Beyond Render and Fly: Zeabur Is Worth a Look

After Heroku killed its free tier, every cloud platform rushed in with free offerings to scoop up those users. Render.com and Fly.io are the usual recommendations, but there's another one worth knowing about — Zeabur, a deployment platform that bills itself on simplicity and "one-click" deploys. This post walks through signing up, deploying an Express app from GitHub, and customizing the domain.
How to Import Images in Vue (Vue CLI and Vite)
A quick note on how to import images properly in Vue CLI or Vite. It's a common question and easy to get wrong, so I figured I'd write it down.
A JavaScript Puzzle: Compute a Math Expression Without Using eval

I came across a fun little JavaScript puzzle the other day, so I jotted down my thought process as a quick post — maybe one day I'll come back and read it myself. The challenge: take a math expression stored as a string and compute its result, but you're not allowed to use eval. We'll look at why eval is a bad idea, and a couple of safer ways to solve it.
About Whether ChatGPT / AI Will Replace Engineers

This was originally a Facebook page post, but I wanted to keep a longer record, so I converted it into an article. The content is slightly tweaked and a bit more substantial (since it's becoming a post).
querySelector vs getElementById: What's the Difference, and the Quirks Nobody Warns You About

querySelector and getElementById are two of the DOM-selecting methods you'll reach for the most in real projects. But what's actually different between them? This post walks through how each one works, the little quirks they hide (case sensitivity, returning null, where you can call them), the performance trade-off, and a handy jQuery-style shortcut.
A Mock API Skill Every Frontend Dev Should Have: Try Mockoon

Every frontend developer should know how to use a Mock API, whether you're senior or junior. This post covers why Mock APIs matter (so you're not stuck waiting on the backend), how to use Mockoon — my go-to Mock API tool — to set up environments, build routes from a spec, configure CORS and ports for frontend integration, and use Faker.js helpers for fake data.
Vue 3 VeeValidate v4.x Advanced: Customising Error Messages
A follow-up to "Vue 3 VeeValidate v4.x Setup Guide" — this one covers customising VeeValidate error messages, since in real-world work you often need to.
What Is Env and When Should You Use It?

A quick rundown of what .env is and when it makes sense to use one. Covers what env vars are for, the common file conventions (.env.development, .env.production, .env.example), how to load them with dotenv, when to use env vars beyond secrets, and the critical caveat that frontend env vars are NOT a place to store sensitive info — they end up in your built bundle.
Why You Shouldn't Call hasOwnProperty Directly (and What ESLint Wants Instead)

ESLint has a rule that yells at you for calling hasOwnProperty directly on an object. At first it feels overly strict, but there's a real reason behind it. This post walks through why obj.hasOwnProperty('key') can break, how an object property can shadow the prototype method, and why Object.prototype.hasOwnProperty.call is the safer way to go.
Auto-Collapsing the Bootstrap RWD Navbar Dropdown on Click
By default, Bootstrap's RWD navbar dropdown doesn't auto-collapse on click. Here's a small trick to make it collapse properly — without breaking nested dropdowns.
